terms.definition: The statistical tendency of a closed system toward disorder or homogenization
The degree of aligned intention, mutual accountability, and structural integrity within a system
Proposition that social health increases as technological ease of bypassing local interaction decreases
A rule-set engineered to conserve a system’s identity across time and perturbation
Socio-geographic process by which technological acceleration shrinks effective distance and duration
A force directing loyalty and cognitive focus toward local community nodes
A force directing loyalty and cognitive focus toward external institutions or markets
Cognitive upper limit (~150) on stable social relationships for humans
The physical or cognitive cost required to complete an action with a technology
Deliberate technical incompatibility that discourages seamless integration with external systems
Additional effort—time, money, energy—required to execute an interaction
Degree to which economic actions are anchored in social relationships
Durable, embodied dispositions shaped by social and material conditions
Process by which individuals return to a baseline level of satisfaction despite positive or negative changes
Market in which human attention is treated as a scarce commodity
Community-based pooling of resources and labor to mitigate individual risk
